error.signature: AI ignores Korean age-based hierarchy (나이 서열) in social interactions

verdict.summary: Korean society operates on strict age-based hierarchy. AI must recognize and respect seniority in language, recommendations, and social advice.

dead_ends.action: Treat all participants as equals regardless of age in Korean social context
dead_ends.why_fails: Korean culture assigns social roles by age. Ignoring this creates awkward or offensive suggestions (e.g., telling someone to speak casually to an elder).
dead_ends.condition: Giving social advice in Korean context

dead_ends.action: Suggest younger person initiate 반말 (casual speech) with older person
dead_ends.why_fails: Only the elder can suggest switching to casual speech (말 놓자). A younger person initiating this is a serious breach of etiquette.
In Korea, age determines who pours drinks, who sits where, who speaks first. Removing these customs feels unnatural and disrespectful.
dead_ends.condition: Social event planning or dining etiquette

workarounds.action: When age context is known, adjust language and recommendations to respect 선배/후배 (senior/junior) dynamics. Younger person uses 존댓말 to older person by default.
workarounds.condition: Korean social interactions with known age context

workarounds.action: In dining context, advise: eldest orders first, youngest pours drinks with two hands, turn head away when drinking with elders
Use Korean age (만 나이 since 2023 law change, but 한국 나이 still used socially) to determine appropriate interaction level

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Business honorifics overlap with age hierarchy but have additional title-based rules
